actionEn: You are on a priority (main) road — you are given priority at intersections, but stay alert.
Priority road status ends here — general rules apply at the next intersections.
Traffic on this road moves in one direction only — do not travel the opposite way.
There is no through route on this road (narrow, dead-end, or for special vehicles) — find another way.
The road on the right has no through route.
The road on the left has no through route.
On a narrow section you have priority — oncoming traffic yields to you.
A pedestrian crossing is here — yield to pedestrians.
A crossing for cyclists — pay attention to them.
A shared crossing for pedestrians and cyclists — yield to both.
An expressway begins — pedestrians, bicycles and slow vehicles are prohibited, special rules apply.
The expressway ends here — normal traffic rules apply again.
A motorway begins — there is a minimum speed, pedestrians, bicycles and tractors are prohibited from entering.
The motorway ends here — speed and traffic rules change.
A lane intended only for buses (sometimes taxis) begins — other traffic is prohibited.
You are in a bus lane — only permitted vehicle types may travel here.
A separate lane for slow-moving vehicles begins.
An additional traffic lane begins.
The additional or slow-vehicle lane ends here — merge into the main flow.
A bus stop — be aware a bus may stop nearby.
A trolleybus stop.
A tram stop — be careful of passengers disembarking.
You may park your vehicle here.
This parking place is reserved — for the specified person or vehicle only.
An enclosed (garage-type) parking area.
This parking place is for taxis only.
The taxi rank ends here.
A hospital is nearby — refrain from sounding your horn, don't disturb the quiet.
A police station is nearby.
A first aid / emergency medical station is nearby.
A petrol/fuel station is nearby.
An LPG/CNG filling station is nearby.
The fuel station also has electric vehicle charging.
An electric vehicle charging station is nearby.
A telephone (emergency contact) is available nearby.
A post office is nearby.
A tyre repair service is nearby.
A car wash is nearby.
A public toilet is nearby.
A shower facility is nearby.
A snack bar or café is nearby.
A restaurant is nearby.
A hotel or motel is nearby.
A campsite (for tents/RVs) is nearby.
The campsite has an electricity supply.
You may temporarily pitch a tent here to rest.
A youth hostel is nearby.
A tourist information point is nearby.
Traffic information can be heard on the stated frequency.
A board with general information (e.g., weather or road conditions).
A pedestrian underpass — do not cross the road above, use the tunnel.
The underpass has a downward escalator.
A pedestrian bridge — cross the road only via the bridge.
The pedestrian bridge has an upward escalator.
A road tunnel begins ahead — turn on your lights, control your speed.
The tunnel ends here.
Information about a toll road ahead.
A residential zone begins — pedestrians have priority, speed is limited to walking pace.
The residential zone ends here — general rules apply again.
You are entering a built-up area — speed drops to the urban limit (usually 50 km/h).
The built-up area ends here — rural road rules apply.
A paid parking zone begins — pay for parking.
The paid parking zone ends here.
This is a private road, its use may depend on the owner's permission.
The private road ends here.
Priority shifts to the other side ahead — approach with care.
Your priority is changing to a yield requirement — be ready.
A toll collection point is ahead — get ready to pay.
There is a special bay on the shoulder for stopping — use it in an emergency.
Automatic speed measurement (speed camera) operates on this section — strictly observe the limit.
Average speed is monitored on this section — do not exceed it over the whole distance.
The average-speed-controlled section ends here.
A mixed traffic zone begins (pedestrians, bicycles, cars) — reduce speed.
The mixed traffic zone ends here.
An environmental zone — older polluting vehicles may be restricted from entering.
The low-emission zone ends here.
